translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'I pray thee, forsake us not, because thou hast known our encamping in the wilderness, and thou hast been to us for eyes;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath come to pass when thou goest with us, yea, it hath come to pass -- that good which Jehovah doth kindly with us -- it we have done kindly to thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from the mount of Jehovah a journey of three days; and the ark of the covenant of Jehovah is journeying before them the journey of three days, to spy out for them a resting-place;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the cloud of Jehovah [is] on them by day, in their journeying from the camp.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass in the journeying of the ark, that Moses saith, 'Rise, O Jehovah, and Thine enemies are scattered, and those hating Thee flee from Thy presence.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in its resting he saith, 'Return, O Jehovah, [to] the myriads, the thousands of Israel.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the people is evil, as those sighing habitually in the ears of Jehovah, and Jehovah heareth, and His anger burneth, and the fire of Jehovah burneth among them, and consumeth in the extremity of the camp.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the people cry unto Moses, and Moses prayeth unto Jehovah, and the fire is quenched;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he calleth the name of that place Taberah, for the fire of Jehovah hath 'burned' among them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the rabble who [are] in its midst have lusted greatly, and the sons of Israel also turn back and weep, and say, 'Who doth give us flesh?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: We have remembered the fish which we do eat in Egypt for nought, the cucumbers, and the melons, and the leeks, and the onions, and the garlick;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now our soul [is] dry, there is not anything, save the manna, before our eyes.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the manna is as coriander seed, and its aspect as the aspect of bdolach;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the people have turned aside and gathered [it], and ground [it] with millstones, or beat [it] in a mortar, and boiled [it] in a pan, and made it cakes, and its taste hath been as the taste of the moisture of oil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in the descending of the dew on the camp by night, the manna descendeth upon it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses heareth the people weeping by its families, each at the opening of his tent, and the anger of Jehovah burneth exceedingly, and in the eyes of Moses [it is] evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith unto Jehovah, 'Why hast Thou done evil to Thy servant? and why have I not found grace in Thine eyes -- to put the burden of all this people upon me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- have I conceived all this people? I -- have I begotten it, that Thou sayest unto me, Carry it in thy bosom as the nursing father beareth the suckling, unto the ground which Thou hast sworn to its fathers?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Whence have I flesh to give to all this people? for they weep unto me, saying, Give to us flesh, and we eat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I am not able -- I alone -- to bear all this people, for [it is] too heavy for me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if thus Thou art doing to me -- slay me, I pray Thee; slay, if I have found grace in thine eyes, and let me not look on mine affliction.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'Gather to Me seventy men of the elders of Israel, whom thou hast known that they are elders of the people, and its authorities; and thou hast taken them unto the tent of meeting, and they have stationed themselves there with thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I have come down and spoken with thee there, and have kept back of the Spirit which [is] upon thee, and have put on them, and they have borne with thee some of the burden of the people, and thou dost not bear [it] thyself alone.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And unto the people thou dost say, Sanctify yourselves for to-morrow, and ye have eaten flesh (for ye have wept in the ears of Jehovah, saying, Who doth give us flesh? for we [had] good in Egypt) -- and Jehovah hath given to you flesh, and ye have eaten.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Ye do not eat one day, nor two days, nor five days, nor ten days, nor twenty days; --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: unto a month of days, till that it come out from your nostrils, and it hath become to you an abomination; because that ye have loathed Jehovah, who [is] in your midst, and weep before Him, saying, Why is this? -- we have come out of Egypt!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith, 'Six hundred thousand footmen [are] the people in whose midst I [am]; and Thou, Thou hast said, Flesh I give to them, and they have eaten, a month of days!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is flock and herd slaughtered for them, that one hath found for them? -- are all the fishes of the sea gathered for them -- that one hath found for them?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'Is the hand of Jehovah become short? now thou dost see whether My word meeteth thee or not.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses goeth out, and speaketh unto the people the words of Jehovah, and gathereth seventy men of the elders of the people, and causeth them to stand round about the tent,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah cometh down in the cloud, and speaketh unto him, and keepeth back of the Spirit which [is] on him, and putteth on the seventy men of the elders; and it cometh to pass at the resting of the Spirit on them, that they prophesy, and do not cease.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And two of the men are left in the camp, the name of the one [is] Eldad, and the name of the second Medad, and the spirit resteth upon them, (and they are among those written, and have not gone out to the tent), and they prophesy in the camp;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the young man runneth, and declareth to Moses, and saith, 'Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joshua son of Nun, minister of Moses, [one] of his young men, answereth and saith, 'My lord Moses, restrain them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith to him, 'Art thou zealous for me? O that all Jehovah's people were prophets! that Jehovah would put His Spirit upon them!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses is gathered unto the camp, he and the elders of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a spirit hath journeyed from Jehovah, and cutteth off quails from the sea, and leaveth by the camp, as a day's journey here, and as a day's journey there, round about the camp, and about two cubits, on the face of the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the people rise all that day, and all the night, and all the day after, and gather the quails -- he who hath least hath gathered ten homers -- and they spread them out for themselves round about the camp.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The flesh is yet between their teeth -- it is not yet cut off -- and the anger of Jehovah hath burned among the people, and Jehovah smiteth among the people -- a very great smiting;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and [one] calleth the name of that place Kibroth-Hattaavah, for there they have buried the people who lust.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From Kibroth-Hattaavah have the people journeyed to Hazeroth, and they are in Hazeroth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Miriam speaketh -- Aaron also -- against Moses concerning the circumstance of the Cushite woman whom he had taken: for a Cushite woman he had taken;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they say, 'Only by Moses hath Jehovah spoken? also by us hath he not spoken?' and Jehovah heareth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man Moses [is] very humble, more than any of the men who [are] on the face of the ground.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith suddenly unto Moses, and unto Aaron, and unto Miriam, 'Come out ye three unto the tent of meeting;' and they three come out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah cometh down in the pillar of the cloud, and standeth at the opening of the tent, and calleth Aaron and Miriam, and they come out both of them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He saith, 'Hear, I pray you, My words: If your prophet is of Jehovah -- in an appearance unto him I make Myself known; in a dream I speak with him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: not so My servant Moses; in all My house he [is] stedfast;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: mouth unto mouth I speak with him, and [by] an appearance, and not in riddles; and the form of Jehovah he beholdeth attentively; and wherefore have ye not been afraid to speak against My servant -- against Moses?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the anger of Jehovah burneth against them, and He goeth on,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the cloud hath turned aside from off the tent, and lo, Miriam [is] leprous as snow; and Aaron turneth unto Miriam, and lo, leprous!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Aaron saith unto Moses, 'O, my lord, I pray thee, lay not upon us sin [in] which we have been foolish, and [in] which we have sinned;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: let her not, I pray thee, be as [one] dead, when in his coming out from the womb of his mother -- the half of his flesh is consumed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses crieth unto Jehovah, saying, 'O God, I pray Thee, give, I pray Thee, healing to her.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'And her father had but spat in her face -- is she not ashamed seven days? she is shut out seven days at the outside of the camp, and afterwards she is gathered.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Miriam is shut out at the outside of the camp seven days, and the people hath not journeyed till Miriam is gathered;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and afterwards have the people journeyed from Hazeroth, and they encamp in the wilderness of Paran.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Send for thee men, and they spy the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the sons of Israel; one man, one man for the tribe of his fathers ye do send, every one a prince among them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses sendeth them from the wilderness of Paran by the command of Jehovah; all of them [are] men, heads of the sons of Israel they are,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and these their names: For the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the tribe of Ephraim, Oshea, son of Nun.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the tribe of Joseph, (for the tribe of Manasseh,) Gaddi son of Susi.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vopshi.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] the names of the men whom Moses hath sent to spy the land; and Moses calleth Hoshea son of Nun, Jehoshua.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses sendeth them to spy the land of Canaan, and saith unto them, 'Go ye up this [way] into the south, and ye have gone up the mountain,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and have seen the land what it [is], and the people which is dwelling on it, whether it [is] strong or feeble; whether it [is] few or many;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and what the land [is] in which it is dwelling, whether it [is] good or bad; and what [are] the cities in which it is dwelling, whether in camps or in fortresses;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And what the land [is], whether it [is] fat or lean; whether there is wood in it or not; and ye have strengthened yourselves, and have taken of the fruit of the land;' and the days [are] days of the first-fruits of grapes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they go up and spy the land, from the wilderness of Zin unto Rehob at the going in to Hamath;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they go up by the south, and come in unto Hebron, and there [are] Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, children of Anak (and Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt),

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they come in unto the brook of Eshcol, and cut down thence a branch and one cluster of grapes, and they bear it on a staff by two, also [some] of the pomegranates, and of the figs.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That place hath [one] called Brook of Eshcol, because of the cluster which the sons of Israel cut from thence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they turn back from spying the land at the end of forty days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they go and come in unto Moses, and unto Aaron, and unto all the company of the sons of Israel, unto the wilderness of Paran, to Kadesh; and they bring them and all the company back word, and shew them the fruit of the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they recount to him, and say, 'We came in unto the land whither thou hast sent us, and also it [is] flowing with milk and honey -- and this [is] its fruit;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: only, surely the people which is dwelling in the land [is] strong; and the cities are fenced, very great; and also children of Anak we have seen there.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Amalek is dwelling in the land of the south, and the Hittite, and the Jebusite, and the Amorite is dwelling in the hill country, and the Canaanite is dwelling by the sea, and by the side of the Jordan.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Caleb stilleth the people concerning Moses, and saith, 'Let us certainly go up -- and we have possessed it; for we are thoroughly able for it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the men who have gone up with him said, 'We are not able to go up against the people, for it [is] stronger than we;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they bring out an evil account of the land which they have spied unto the sons of Israel, saying, 'The land into which we passed over to spy it, is a land eating up its inhabitants; and all the people whom we saw in its midst [are] men of stature;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there we saw the Nephilim, sons of Anak, of the Nephilim; and we are in our own eyes as grasshoppers; and so we were in their eyes.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the company lifteth up and give forth their voice, and the people weep during that night;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the sons of Israel murmur against Moses, and against Aaron, and all the company say unto them, 'O that we had died in the land of Egypt, or in this wilderness, O that we had died!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and why is Jehovah bringing us in unto this land to fall by the sword? our wives and our infants are become a prey; is it not good for us to turn back to Egypt?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say one unto another, 'Let us appoint a head, and turn back to Egypt.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses falleth -- Aaron also -- on their faces, before all the assembly of the company of the sons of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joshua son of Nun, and Caleb son of Jephunneh, of those spying the land, have rent their garments,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they speak unto all the company of the sons of Israel, saying, 'The land into which we have passed over to spy it, [is] a very very good land;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if Jehovah hath delighted in us, then He hath brought us in unto this land, and hath given it to us, a land which is flowing with milk and honey;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: only, against Jehovah rebel not ye: and ye, fear not ye the people of the land, for our bread they [are]; their defence hath turned aside from off them, and Jehovah [is] with us; fear them not.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the company say to stone them with stones, and the honour of Jehovah hath appeared in the tent of meeting unto all the sons of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'Until when doth this people despise Me? and until when do they not believe in Me, for all the signs which I have done in its midst?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I smite it with pestilence, and dispossess it, and make thee become a nation greater and mightier than it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith unto Jehovah, 'Then have the Egyptians heard! for Thou hast brought up with Thy power this people out of their midst,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they have said [it] unto the inhabitant of this land, they have heard that Thou, Jehovah, [art] in the midst of this people, that eye to eye Thou art seen -- O Jehovah, and Thy cloud is standing over them, -- and in a pillar of cloud Thou art going before them by day, and in a pillar of fire by night.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Thou hast put to death this people as one man, and the nations who have heard Thy fame have spoken, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From Jehovah's want of ability to bring in this people unto the land which He hath sworn to them -- He doth slaughter them in the wilderness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, let, I pray Thee, the power of my Lord be great, as Thou hast spoken, saying:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah [is] slow to anger, and of great kindness; bearing away iniquity and transgression, and not entirely acquitting, charging iniquity of fathers on sons, on a third [generation], and on a fourth; --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: forgive, I pray Thee, the iniquity of this people, according to the greatness of Thy kindness, and as Thou hast borne with this people from Egypt, even until now.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith, 'I have forgiven, according to thy word;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and yet, I live -- and it is filled -- the whole earth -- [with] the honour of Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for all the men who are seeing My honour, and My signs, which I have done in Egypt, and in the wilderness, and try Me these ten times, and have not hearkened to My voice --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they see not the land which I have sworn to their fathers, yea, none of those despising Me see it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and My servant Caleb, because there hath been another spirit with him, and he is fully after Me -- I have brought him in unto the land whither he hath entered, and his seed doth possess it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the Amalekite and the Canaanite are dwelling in the valley; to-morrow turn ye and journey for yourselves into the wilderness -- the way of the Red Sea.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, and unto Aaron, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Until when hath this evil company that which they are murmuring against Me? the murmurings of the sons of Israel, which they are murmuring against Me, I have heard;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: say unto them, I live -- an affirmation of Jehovah -- if, as ye have spoken in Mine ears -- so I do not to you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in this wilderness do your carcases fall, even all your numbered ones, to all your number, from a son of twenty years and upward, who have murmured against Me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: ye -- ye come not in unto the land which I have lifted up My hand to cause you to tabernacle in it, except Caleb son of Jephunneh, and Joshua son of Nun.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'As to your infants -- of whom ye have said, A spoil they are become -- I have even brought them in, and they have known the land which ye have kicked against;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as to you -- your carcases do fall in this wilderness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and your sons are evil in the wilderness forty years, and have borne your whoredoms till your carcases are consumed in the wilderness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: by the number of the days [in] which ye spied the land, forty days, -- a day for a year, a day for a year -- ye do bear your iniquities, forty years, and ye have known my breaking off;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I [am] Jehovah, I have spoken; if I do not this to all this evil company who are meeting against me; -- in this wilderness they are consumed, and there they die.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the men whom Moses hath sent to spy the land, and they turn back and cause all the company to murmur against him, by bringing out an evil account concerning the land,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: even the men bringing out an evil account of the land die by the plague before Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joshua son of Nun, and Caleb son of Jephunneh, have lived of those men who go to spy out the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses speaketh these words unto all the sons of Israel, and the people mourn exceedingly,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they rise early in the morning, and go up unto the top of the mountain, saying, 'Here we [are], and we have come up unto the place which Jehovah hath spoken of, for we have sinned.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith, 'Why [is] this? -- ye are transgressing the command of Jehovah, and it doth not prosper;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: go not up, for Jehovah is not in your midst, and ye are not smitten before your enemies;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the Amalekite and the Canaanite [are] there before you, and ye have fallen by the sword, because that ye have turned back from after Jehovah, and Jehovah is not with you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they presume to go up unto the top of the mountain, and the ark of the covenant of Jehovah and Moses have not departed out of the midst of the camp.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Amalekite and the Canaanite who are dwelling in that mountain come down and smite them, and beat them down -- unto Hormah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou hast said unto them, When ye come in unto the land of your dwellings, which I am giving to you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then ye have prepared a fire-offering to Jehovah, a burnt-offering, or a sacrifice, at separating a vow or free-will-offering, or in your appointed things, to make a sweet fragrance to Jehovah, out of the herd, or out of the flock.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he who is bringing near his offering to Jehovah hath brought near a present of flour, a tenth deal, mixed with a fourth of the hin of oil;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and wine for a libation, a fourth of the hin thou dost prepare for the burnt-offering or for a sacrifice, for the one lamb;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: or for a ram thou dost prepare a present of flour, two-tenth deals, mixed with oil, a third of the hin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and wine for a libation, a third part of the hin, thou dost bring near -- a sweet fragrance to Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when thou makest a son of the herd a burnt-offering or a sacrifice, at separating a vow or peace-offerings to Jehovah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then he hath brought near for the son of the herd a present of flour, three-tenth deals, mixed with oil, a half of the hin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and wine thou bringest near for a libation, a half of the hin -- a fire-offering of sweet fragrance to Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thus it is done for the one ox, or for the one ram, or for a lamb of the sheep or of the goats.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'According to the number that ye prepare, so ye do to each, according to their number;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: every native doth thus with these, at bringing near a fire-offering of sweet fragrance to Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when a sojourner sojourneth with you, or whoso [is] in your midst to your generations, and he hath made a fire-offering of sweet fragrance to Jehovah, as ye do so he doth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'One statute is for you of the congregation and for the sojourner who is sojourning, a statute age-during to your generations: as ye [are] so is the sojourner before Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: one law and one ordinance is to you and to the sojourner who is sojourning with you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou hast said unto them, In your coming in unto the land whither I am bringing you in,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then it hath been, in your eating of the bread of the land, ye heave up a heave-offering to Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the beginning of your dough a cake ye heave up -- a heave-offering; as the heave-offering of a threshing-floor, so ye do heave it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Of the beginning of your dough ye do give to Jehovah a heave-offering -- to your generations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when ye err, and do not all these commands which Jehovah hath spoken unto Moses,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the whole that Jehovah hath charged upon you by the hand of Moses, from the day that Jehovah hath commanded, and henceforth, to your generations,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then it hath been, if from the eyes of the company it hath been done in ignorance, that all the company have prepared one bullock, a son of the herd, for a burnt-offering, for sweet fragrance to Jehovah, and its present, and its libation, according to the ordinance, and one kid of the goats for a sin-offering.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the priest hath made atonement for all the company of the sons of Israel, and it hath been forgiven them, for it [is] ignorance, and they -- they have brought in their offering, a fire-offering to Jehovah, even their sin-offering before Jehovah for their ignorance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been forgiven to all the company of the sons of Israel, and to the sojourner who is sojourning in their midst; for to all the company [it is done] in ignorance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if one person sin in ignorance, then he hath brought near a she-goat, daughter of a year, for a sin-offering;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the priest hath made atonement for the person who is erring, in his sinning in ignorance before Jehovah, by making atonement for him, and it hath been forgiven him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the native among the sons of Israel, and for the sojourner who is sojourning in their midst -- one law is to you, for him who is doing [anything] through ignorance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the person who doth [aught] with a high hand -- of the native or of the sojourner -- Jehovah he is reviling, and that person hath been cut off from the midst of his people;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because the word of Jehovah he despised, and His command hath broken -- that person is certainly cut off; his iniquity [is] on him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Israel are in the wilderness, and they find a man gathering wood on the sabbath-day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those finding him gathering wood bring him near unto Moses, and unto Aaron, and unto all the company,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they place him in ward, for it [is] not explained what is [to be] done to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'The man is certainly put to death, all the company stoning him with stones, at the outside of the camp.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the company bring him out unto the outside of the camp, and stone him with stones, and he dieth, as Jehovah hath commanded Moses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou hast said unto them, and they have made for themselves fringes on the skirts of their garments, to their generations, and they have put on the fringe of the skirt a ribbon of blue,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been to you for a fringe, and ye have seen it, and have remembered all the commands of Jehovah, and have done them, and ye search not after your heart, and after your eyes, after which ye are going a-whoring;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so that ye remember and have done all My commands, and ye have been holy to your God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I [am] Jehovah your God, who hath brought you out from the land of Egypt to become your God; I, Jehovah, [am] your God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Korah, son of Izhar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, taketh both Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab, and On son of Peleth, sons of Reuben,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they rise up before Moses, with men of the sons of Israel, two hundred and fifty, princes of the company, called of the convention, men of name,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they are assembled against Moses and against Aaron, and say unto them, 'Enough of you! for all the company -- all of them [are] holy, and in their midst [is] Jehovah; and wherefore do ye lift yourselves up above the assembly of Jehovah?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses heareth, and falleth on his face,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he speaketh unto Korah, and unto all his company, saying, 'Morning! -- and Jehovah is knowing those who are his, and him who is holy, and hath brought near unto Him; even him whom He doth fix on He bringeth near unto Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This do: take to yourselves censers, Korah, and all his company,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and put in them fire, and put on them perfume, before Jehovah to-morrow, and it hath been, the man whom Jehovah chooseth, he [is] the holy one; -- enough of you, sons of Levi.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith unto Korah, 'Hear ye, I pray you, sons of Levi;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: is it little to you that the God of Israel hath separated you from the company of Israel to bring you near unto Himself, to do the service of the tabernacle of Jehovah, and to stand before the company to serve them? --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: yea, He doth bring thee near, and all thy brethren the sons of Levi with thee -- and ye have sought also the priesthood!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore, thou and all thy company who are met [are] against Jehovah; and Aaron, what [is] he, that ye murmur against him?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses sendeth to call for Dathan and for Abiram sons of Eliab, and they say, 'We do not come up;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: is it little that thou hast brought us up out of a land flowing with milk and honey to put us to death in a wilderness that thou also certainly makest thyself prince over us?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, unto a land flowing with milk and honey thou hast not brought us in, nor dost thou give to us an inheritance of field and vineyard; the eyes of these men dost thou pick out? we do not come up.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it is very displeasing to Moses, and he saith unto Jehovah, 'Turn not Thou unto their present; not one ass from them have I taken, nor have I afflicted one of them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith unto Korah, 'Thou and all thy company, be ye before Jehovah, thou, and they, and Aaron, to-morrow;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and take ye each his censer, and ye have put on them perfume, and brought near before Jehovah, each his censer, two hundred and fifty censers; and thou and Aaron, each his censer.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they take each his censer, and put on them fire, and lay on them perfume, and they stand at the opening of the tent of meeting, with Moses and Aaron.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Korah assembleth against them all the company unto the opening of the tent of meeting, and the honour of Jehovah is seen by all the company.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Be ye separated from the midst of this company, and I consume them in a moment;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they fall on their faces, and say, 'God, God of the spirits of all flesh -- the one man sinneth, and against all the company Thou art wroth!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Speak unto the company, saying, Go ye up from round about the tabernacle of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses riseth, and goeth unto Dathan and Abiram, and the elders of Israel go after him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he speaketh unto the company, saying, 'Turn aside, I pray you, from the tents of these wicked men, and come not against anything that they have, lest ye be consumed in all their sins.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they go up from the tabernacle of Korah, Dathan and Abiram, from round about, and Dathan, and Abiram have come out, standing at the opening of their tents, and their wives, and their sons, and their infants.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith, 'By this ye do know that Jehovah hath sent me to do all these works, that [they are] not from my own heart;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if according to the death of all men these die -- or the charge of all men is charged upon them -- Jehovah hath not sent me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if a strange thing Jehovah do, and the ground hath opened her mouth and swallowed them, and all that they have, and they have gone down alive to Sheol -- then ye have known that these men have despised Jehovah.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass at his finishing speaking all these words, that the ground which [is] under them cleaveth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the earth openeth her mouth, and swalloweth them, and their houses, and all the men who [are] for Korah, and all the goods,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they go down, they, and all that they have, alive to Sheol, and the earth closeth over them, and they perish from the midst of the assembly;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all Israel who [are] round about them have fled at their voice, for they said, 'Lest the earth swallow us;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and fire hath come out from Jehovah, and consumeth the two hundred and fifty men bringing near the perfume.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Say unto Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, and he lifteth up the censers from the midst of the burning, and the fire scatter thou yonder, for they have been hallowed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: [even] the censers of these sinners against their own souls; and they have made them spread-out plates, a covering for the altar, for they have brought them near before Jehovah, and they are hallowed; and they are become a sign to the sons of Israel.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Eleazar the priest taketh the brazen censers which they who are burnt had brought near, and they spread them out, a covering for the altar --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a memorial to the sons of Israel, so that a stranger who is not of the seed of Aaron doth not draw near to make a perfume before Jehovah, and is not as Korah, and as his company, -- as Jehovah hath spoken by the hand of Moses to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the company of the sons of Israel murmur, on the morrow, against Moses and against Aaron, saying, 'Ye -- ye have put to death the people of Jehovah.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, in the company being assembled against Moses and against Aaron, that they turn towards the tent of meeting, and lo, the cloud hath covered it, and the honour of Jehovah is seen;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses cometh -- Aaron also -- unto the front of the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Get you up from the midst of this company, and I consume them in a moment;' and they fall on their faces,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses saith unto Aaron, 'Take the censer, and put on it fire from off the altar, and place perfume, and go, hasten unto the company, and make atonement for them, for the wrath hath gone out from the presence of Jehovah -- the plague hath begun.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Aaron taketh as Moses hath spoken, and runneth unto the midst of the assembly, and lo, the plague hath begun among the people; and he giveth the perfume, and maketh atonement for the people,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and standeth between the dead and the living, and the plague is restrained;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those who die by the plague are fourteen thousand and seven hundred, apart from those who die for the matter of Korah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Aaron turneth back unto Moses, unto the opening of the tent of meeting, and the plague hath been restrained.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Speak unto the sons of Israel, and take from them each a rod, for a father's house, from all their princes, for the house of their fathers, twelve rods; the name of each thou dost write on his rod,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Aaron's name thou dost write on the tribe of Levi; for one rod [is] for the head of their fathers' house:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast placed them in the tent of meeting, before the testimony, where I meet with you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath come to pass, the man's rod on whom I fix doth flourish, and I have caused to cease from off me the murmurings of the sons of Israel, which they are murmuring against you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses speaketh unto the sons of Israel, and all their princes give unto him one rod for a prince, one rod for a prince, for their fathers' house, twelve rods, and the rod of Aaron [is] in the midst of their rods;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses placeth the rods before Jehovah, in the tent of the testimony.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, on the morrow, that Moses goeth in unto the tent of the testimony, and lo, the rod of Aaron hath flourished for the house of Levi, and is bringing out flourishing, and doth blossom blossoms, and doth produce almonds;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses bringeth out all the rods from before Jehovah, unto all the sons of Israel, and they look, and take each his rod.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'Put back the rod of Aaron, before the testimony, for a charge, for a sign to the sons of rebellion, and thou dost remove their murmurings from off me, and they do not die;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses doth as Jehovah hath commanded him; so he hath done.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Israel speak unto Moses, saying, 'Lo, we have expired; we have perished; we have all of us perished;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: any who is at all drawing near unto the tabernacle of Jehovah dieth; have we not been consumed -- to expire?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Aaron, 'Thou, and thy sons, and the house of thy father with thee, do bear the iniquity of the sanctuary; and thou, and thy sons with thee, do bear the iniquity of your priesthood;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and also thy brethren, the tribe of Levi, the tribe of thy father, bring near with thee, and they are joined unto thee, and serve thee, even thou and thy sons with thee, before the tent of the testimony.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And they have kept thy charge, and the charge of all the tent; only, unto the vessels of the sanctuary and unto the altar they do not come near, and they die not, either they or you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they have been joined unto thee, and have kept the charge of the tent of meeting, for all the service of the tent; and a stranger doth not come near unto you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have kept the charge of the sanctuary, and the charge of the altar, and there is no more wrath against the sons of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I, lo, I have taken your brethren the Levites from the midst of the sons of Israel; to you a gift they are given by Jehovah, to do the service of the tent of meeting;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou, and thy sons with thee, do keep your priesthood, for everything of the altar, and within the vail, and ye have served; a service of gift I make your priesthood; and the stranger who is coming near is put to death.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Aaron: 'And I, lo, I have given to thee the charge of My heave-offerings, of all the hallowed things of the sons of Israel -- to thee I have given them for the anointing, and to thy sons, by a statute age-during.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This is thine of the most holy things, from the fire: all their offering, to all their present, and to all their sin-offering, and to all their guilt-offering, which they give back to Me, is most holy to thee, and to thy sons;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in the holy of holies thou dost eat it; every male doth eat it; holy it is to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And this [is] thine: the heave-offering of their gift, to all the wave-offerings of the sons of Israel, to thee I have given them, and to thy sons, and to thy daughters with thee, by a statute age-during; every clean one in thy house doth eat it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: all the best of the oil, and all the best of the new wine, and wheat -- their first -[fruits] which they give to Jehovah -- to thee I have given them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The first-fruits of all that [is] in their land, which they bring in to Jehovah, are thine; every clean one in thy house doth eat it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: every devoted thing in Israel is thine,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: every one opening a womb of all flesh which they bring near to Jehovah, among man and among beast, is thine; only, thou dost certainly ransom the first-born of man, and the firstling of the unclean beast thou dost ransom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And their ransomed ones from a son of a month, thou dost ransom with thy valuation, of silver, five shekels, by the shekel of the sanctuary, twenty gerahs it [is].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only, the firstling of a cow, or the firstling of a sheep, or the firstling of a goat, thou dost not ransom, holy they [are]: their blood thou dost sprinkle on the altar, and of their fat thou makest perfume, a fire-offering of sweet fragrance to Jehovah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their flesh is thine, as the breast of the wave-offering, and as the right leg, it is thine;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: all the heave-offerings of the holy things which the sons of Israel lift up to Jehovah I have given to thee and to thy sons, and to thy daughters with thee, by a statute age-during, a covenant of salt, age-during it [is] before Jehovah, to thee and to thy seed with thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Aaron, 'In their land thou dost not inherit, and a portion thou hast not in their midst: I [am] thy portion, and thine inheritance in the midst of the sons of Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to the sons of Levi, lo, I have given all the tenth in Israel for inheritance in exchange for their service which they are serving -- the service of the tent of meeting.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the sons of Israel come no more near unto the tent of meeting, to bear sin, to die,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Levites have done the service of the tent of meeting, and they -- they bear their iniquity; a statute age-during to your generations, that in the midst of the sons of Israel they have no inheritance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the tithe of the sons of Israel which they lift up to Jehovah, a heave-offering, I have given to the Levites for inheritance; therefore I have said of them, In the midst of the sons of Israel they have no inheritance.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And unto the Levites thou dost speak; and thou hast said unto them, When ye take from the sons of Israel the tithe which I have given to you from them, for your inheritance, then ye have lifted up from it the heave-offering of Jehovah, a tithe of the tithe;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and your heave-offering hath been reckoned to you as corn from the threshing-floor, and as fulness from the wine-vat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so ye do lift up -- ye also -- the heave-offering of Jehovah from all your tithes which ye receive from the sons of Israel; and ye have given from it the heave-offering of Jehovah to Aaron the priest;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: out of all your gifts ye do lift up the whole heave-offering of Jehovah; out of all its fat, -- its hallowed part -- out of it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou hast said unto them, In your lifting up its fat out of it, then it hath been reckoned to the Levites, as increase of a threshing-floor, and as increase of a wine-vat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have eaten it in every place, ye and your households, for it [is] your hire in exchange for your service in the tent of meeting;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye bear no sin for it, in your lifting up its fat out of it, and the holy things of the sons of Israel ye do not pollute, and ye die not.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, and unto Aaron, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'This [is] a statute of the law which Jehovah hath commanded, saying, Speak unto the sons of Israel, and they bring unto thee a red cow, a perfect one, in which there is no blemish, on which no yoke hath gone up;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have given it unto Eleazar the priest, and he hath brought it out unto the outside of the camp, and hath slaughtered it before him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Eleazar the priest hath taken of its blood with his finger, and hath sprinkled over-against the front of the tent of meeting of her blood seven times;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and [one] hath burnt the cow before his eyes; her skin, and her flesh, and her blood, besides her dung, he doth burn;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the priest hath taken cedar wood, and hyssop, and scarlet, and hath cast unto the midst of the burning of the cow;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the priest hath washed his garments, and hath bathed his flesh with water, and afterwards doth come in unto the camp, and the priest is unclean till the evening;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he who is burning it doth wash his garments with water, and hath bathed his flesh with water, and is unclean till the evening.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And a clean man hath gathered the ashes of the cow, and hath placed at the outside of the camp, in a clean place, and it hath become to the company of the sons of Israel a charge for waters of separation -- it [is] a [cleansing];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he who is gathering the ashes of the heifer hath washed his garments, and is unclean till the evening; and it hath been to the sons of Israel, and to the sojourner who is sojourning in their midst, for a statute age-during.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'He who is coming against the dead body of any man -- is unclean seven days;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he doth cleanse himself for it on the third day, and on the seventh day he is clean; and if he cleanse not himself on the third day, then on the seventh day he is not clean.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Any one who is coming against the dead, against the body of man who dieth, and cleanseth not himself -- the tabernacle of Jehovah he hath defiled, and that person hath been cut off from Israel, for water of separation is not sprinkled upon him; he is unclean; his uncleanness [is] still upon him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'This [is] the law, when a man dieth in a tent: every one who is coming in unto the tent, and all that [is] in the tent, is unclean seven days;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and every open vessel which hath no covering of thread upon it is unclean.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And every one who cometh, on the face of the field, against the pierced of a sword, or against the dead, or against a bone of man, or against a grave, is unclean seven days;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they have taken for the unclean person of the ashes of the burning of the [cleansing], and he hath put upon it running water unto a vessel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a clean person hath taken hyssop, and hath dipped [it] in water, and hath sprinkled on the tent, and on all the vessels, and on the persons who have been there, and on him who is coming against a bone, or against one pierced, or against the dead, or against a grave.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the clean hath sprinkled [it] on the unclean on the third day, and on the seventh day, and hath cleansed him on the seventh day, and he hath washed his garments, and hath bathed with water, and hath been clean in the evening.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the man who is unclean, and doth not cleanse himself, even that person hath been cut off from the midst of the assembly; for the sanctuary of Jehovah he hath defiled; water of separation is not sprinkled upon him; he [is] unclean.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath been to them for a statute age-during, that he who is sprinkling the water of separation doth wash his garments, and he who is coming against the water of separation is unclean till the evening,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all against which the unclean person cometh is unclean, and the person who is coming against [it] is unclean till the evening.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Israel come in, -- all the company -- to the wilderness of Zin, in the first month, and the people abide in Kadesh, and Miriam dieth there, and is buried there.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there hath been no water for the company, and they are assembled against Moses, and against Aaron,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the people strive with Moses, and speak, saying, 'And oh that we had expired when our brethren expired before Jehovah!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and why have ye brought in the assembly of Jehovah unto this wilderness to die there, we and our beasts?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and why hast thou brought us up out of Egypt to bring us in unto this evil place? no place of seed, and fig, and vine, and pomegranate; and water there is none to drink.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses and Aaron go in from the presence of the assembly unto the opening of the tent of meeting, and fall on their faces, and the honour of Jehovah is seen by them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take the rod, and assemble the company, thou and Aaron thy brother; and ye have spoken unto the rock before their eyes, and it hath given its water, and thou hast brought out to them water from the rock, and hast watered the company, and their beasts.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses taketh the rod from before Jehovah, as He hath commanded him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses and Aaron assemble the assembly unto the front of the rock, and he saith to them, 'Hear, I pray you, O rebels, from this rock do we bring out to you water?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses lifteth up his hand, and smiteth the rock with his rod twice; and much water cometh out, and the company drink, also their beasts.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, and unto Aaron, 'Because ye have not believed in Me to sanctify Me before the eyes of the sons of Israel, therefore ye do not bring in this assembly unto the land which I have given to them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] waters of Meribah, because the sons of Israel have 'striven' with Jehovah, and He is sanctified upon them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses sendeth messengers from Kadesh unto the king of Edom, 'Thus said thy brother Israel, Thou -- thou hast known all the travail which hath found us;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that our fathers go down to Egypt, and we dwell in Egypt many days, and the Egyptians do evil to us and to our fathers;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we cry unto Jehovah, and He heareth our voice, and sendeth a messenger, and is bringing us out of Egypt; and lo, we [are] in Kadesh, a city [in] the extremity of thy border.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let us pass over, we pray thee, through thy land; we pass not over through a field, or through a vineyard, nor do we drink waters of a well; the way of the king we go, we turn not aside -- right or left -- till that we pass over thy border.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Edom saith unto him, 'Thou dost not pass over through me, lest with sword I come out to meet thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Israel say unto him, 'In the highway we go, and if of thy waters we drink -- I and my cattle -- then I have given their price; only (it is nothing) on my feet I pass over.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Thou dost not pass over;' and Edom cometh out to meet him with much people, and with a strong hand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Edom refuseth to suffer Israel to pass over through his border, and Israel turneth aside from off him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Israel, the whole company, journey from Kadesh, and come in unto mount Hor,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah speaketh unto Moses and unto Aaron in mount Hor, on the border of the land of Edom, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Aaron is gathered unto his people, for he doth not go in unto the land which I have given to the sons of Israel, because that ye provoked My mouth at the waters of Meribah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take Aaron and Eleazar his son, and cause them to go up mount Hor,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and strip Aaron of his garments, and thou hast clothed [with] them Eleazar his son, and Aaron is gathered, and doth die there.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses doth as Jehovah hath commanded, and they go up unto mount Hor before the eyes of all the company,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses strippeth Aaron of his garments, and clotheth with them Eleazar his son, and Aaron dieth there on the top of the mount; and Moses cometh down -- Eleazar also -- from the mount,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the company see that Aaron hath expired, and they bewail Aaron thirty days -- all the house of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Canaanite -- king Arad -- dwelling in the south, heareth that Israel hath come the way of the Atharim, and he fighteth against Israel, and taketh [some] of them captive.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel voweth a vow to Jehovah, and saith, 'If Thou dost certainly give this people into my hand, then I have devoted their cities;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah hearkeneth to the voice of Israel, and giveth up the Canaanite, and he devoteth them and their cities, and calleth the name of the place Hormah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from mount Hor, the way of the Red Sea, to compass the land of Edom, and the soul of the people is short in the way,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the people speak against God, and against Moses, 'Why hast thou brought us up out of Egypt to die in a wilderness? for there is no bread, and there is no water, and our soul hath been weary of this light bread.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah sendeth among the people the burning serpents, and they bite the people, and much people of Israel die;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the people come in unto Moses and say, 'We have sinned, for we have spoken against Jehovah, and against thee; pray unto Jehovah, and He doth turn aside from us the serpent;' and Moses prayeth in behalf of the people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'Make for thee a burning [serpent], and set it on an ensign; and it hath been, every one who is bitten and hath seen it -- he hath lived.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses maketh a serpent of brass, and setteth it on the ensign, and it hath been, if the serpent hath bitten any man, and he hath looked expectingly unto the serpent of brass -- he hath lived.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Israel journey, and encamp in Oboth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Oboth, and encamp in Ije-Abarim, in the wilderness that [is] on the front of Moab, at the rising of the sun.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From thence they have journeyed, and encamp in the valley of Zared.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From thence they have journeyed, and encamp beyond Arnon, which [is] in the wilderness which is coming out of the border of the Amorite, for Arnon [is] the border of Moab, between Moab and the Amorite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore it is said in a book, 'The wars of Jehovah,' -- 'Waheb in Suphah, And the brooks of Arnon;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the spring of the brooks, Which turned aside to the dwelling of Ar, And hath leaned to the border of Moab.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And from thence [they journeyed] to Beer; it [is] the well [concerning] which Jehovah said to Moses, 'Gather the people, and I give to them -- water.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then singeth Israel this song, concerning the well -- they have answered to it:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'A well -- digged it have princes, Prepared it have nobles of the people, With the lawgiver, with their staves.' And from the wilderness [they journeyed] to Mattanah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and from Mattanah to Nahaliel, and from Nahaliel to Bamoth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and from Bamoth in the valley which [is] in the field of Moab [to] the top of Pisgah, which hath looked on the front of the wilderness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel sendeth messengers unto Sihon king of the Amorite, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Let me pass through thy land, we do not turn aside into a field, or into a vineyard, we do not drink waters of a well; in the king's way we go, till that we pass over thy border.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Sihon hath not suffered Israel to pass through his border, and Sihon gathereth all his people, and cometh out to meet Israel into the wilderness, and cometh in to Jahaz, and fighteth against Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel smiteth him by the mouth of the sword, and possesseth his land from Arnon unto Jabbok -- unto the sons of Ammon; for the border of the sons of Ammon [is] strong.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel taketh all these cities, and Israel dwelleth in all the cities of the Amorite, in Heshbon, and in all its villages;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Heshbon is a city of Sihon king of the Amorite, and he hath fought against the former king of Moab, and taketh all his land out of his hand, unto Arnon;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore those using similes say -- 'Enter ye Heshbon, Let the city of Sihon be built and ready,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For fire hath gone out from Heshbon, A flame from the city of Sihon, It hath consumed Ar of Moab, Owners of the high places of Arnon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Woe to thee, O Moab, Thou hast perished, O people of Chemosh, He hath given his sons who escape -- Also his daughters -- Into captivity, to a king of the Amorite -- Sihon!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And we shoot them, Perished hath Heshbon unto Dibon, And we make desolate unto Nophah, Which [is] unto Medeba.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel dwelleth in the land of the Amorite,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses sendeth to spy out Jaazer, and they capture its villages, and dispossess the Amorite who [is] there,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and turn and go up the way of Bashan, and Og king of Bashan cometh out to meet them, he and all his people, to battle, [at] Edrei.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'Fear him not, for into thy hand I have given him, and all his people, and his land, and thou hast done to him as thou hast done to Sihon king of the Amorite, who is dwelling in Heshbon.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they smite him, and his sons, and all his people, until he hath not left to him a remnant, and they possess his land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Israel journey and encamp in the plains of Moab, beyond the Jordan, [by] Jericho.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balak son of Zippor seeth all that Israel hath done to the Amorite,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moab is exceedingly afraid of the presence of the people, for it [is] numerous; and Moab is vexed by the presence of the sons of Israel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moab saith unto the elders of Midian, 'Now doth the assembly lick up all that is round about us, as the ox licketh up the green thing of the field.' And Balak son of Zippor [is] king of Moab at that time,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he sendeth messengers unto Balaam son of Beor, to Pethor, which [is] by the River of the land of the sons of his people, to call for him, saying, 'Lo, a people hath come out of Egypt; lo, it hath covered the eye of the land, and it is abiding over-against me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, come, I pray thee, curse for me this people, for it [is] mightier than I; it may be I prevail -- we smite it -- and I cast it out from the land; for I have known -- that which thou blessest is blessed, and that which thou cursest is cursed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the elders of Moab and the elders of Midian go, and divinations in their hand, and they come in unto Balaam, and speak unto him the words of Balak,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith unto them, 'Lodge here to-night, and I have brought you back word, as Jehovah speaketh unto me;' and the princes of Moab abide with Balaam.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God cometh in unto Balaam, and saith, 'Who [are] these men with thee?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balaam saith unto God, 'Balak, son of Zippor, king of Moab, hath sent unto me:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, the people that is coming out from Egypt and covereth the eye of the land, -- now come, pierce it for me; it may be I am able to fight against it, and have cast it out;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God saith unto Balaam, 'Thou dost not go with them; thou dost not curse the people; for it [is] blessed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balaam riseth in the morning, and saith unto the princes of Balak, 'Go unto your land, for Jehovah is refusing to suffer me to go with you;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the princes of Moab rise, and come in unto Balak, and say, 'Balaam is refusing to come with us.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balak addeth yet to send princes, more numerous and honoured than these,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they come in unto Balaam, and say to him, 'Thus said Balak son of Zippor, Be not, I pray thee, withheld from coming unto me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for very greatly I honour thee, and all that thou sayest unto me I do; and come, I pray thee, pierce for me this people.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balaam answereth and saith unto the servants of Balak, 'If Balak doth give to me the fulness of his house of silver and gold, I am not able to pass over the command of Jehovah my God, to do a little or a great thing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and, now, abide, I pray you, in this [place], you also, to-night; and I know what Jehovah is adding to speak with me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God cometh in unto Balaam, by night, and saith to him, 'If to call for thee the men have come, rise, go with them, and only the thing which I speak unto thee -- it thou dost do.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balaam riseth in the morning, and saddleth his ass, and goeth with the princes of Moab,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the anger of God burneth because he is going, and a messenger of Jehovah stationeth himself in the way for an adversary to him, and he is riding on his ass, and two of his servants [are] with him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the ass seeth the messenger of Jehovah standing in the way, and his drawn sword in his hand, and the ass turneth aside out of the way, and goeth into a field, and Balaam smiteth the ass to turn it aside into the way.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messenger of Jehovah standeth in a narrow path of the vineyards -- a wall on this [side] and a wall on that --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the ass seeth the messenger of Jehovah, and is pressed unto the wall, and presseth Balaam's foot unto the wall, and he addeth to smite her;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
