translations.text: and the messenger of Jehovah addeth to pass over, and standeth in a strait place where there is no way to turn aside -- right or left --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the ass seeth the messenger of Jehovah, and croucheth under Balaam, and the anger of Balaam burneth, and he smiteth the ass with a staff.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah openeth the mouth of the ass, and she saith to Balaam, 'What have I done to thee that thou hast smitten me these three times?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Balaam saith to the ass, 'Because thou hast rolled thyself against me; oh that there were a sword in my hand, for now I had slain thee;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the ass saith unto Balaam, 'Am not I thine ass, upon which thou hast ridden since [I was] thine unto this day? have I at all been accustomed to do to thee thus?' and he saith, 'No.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah uncovereth the eyes of Balaam, and he seeth the messenger of Jehovah standing in the way, and his drawn sword in his hand, and he boweth and doth obeisance, to his face;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the messenger of Jehovah saith unto him, 'Wherefore hast thou smitten thine ass these three times? lo, I -- I have come out for an adversary, for [thy] way hath been perverse before me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the ass seeth me, and turneth aside at my presence these three times; unless she had turned aside from my presence, surely now also, thee I had slain, and her kept alive.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balaam saith unto the messenger of Jehovah, 'I have sinned, for I did not know that thou [art] standing to meet me in the way; and now, if evil in thine eyes -- I turn back by myself.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messenger of Jehovah saith unto Balaam, 'Go with the men; and only the word which I speak unto thee -- it thou dost speak;' and Balaam goeth with the princes of Balak.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balak heareth that Balaam hath come, and goeth out to meet him, unto a city of Moab, which [is] on the border of Arnon, which [is] in the extremity of the border;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Balak saith unto Balaam, 'Did I not diligently sent unto thee to call for thee? why didst thou not come unto me? am I not truly able to honour thee?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balaam saith unto Balak, 'Lo, I have come unto thee; now -- am I at all able to speak anything? the word which God setteth in my mouth -- it I do speak.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balaam goeth with Balak, and they come to Kirjath-Huzoth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Balak sacrificeth oxen and sheep, and sendeth to Balaam, and to the princes who [are] with him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass in the morning, that Balak taketh Balaam, and causeth him to go up the high places of Baal, and he seeth from thence the extremity of the people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balaam saith unto Balak, 'Build for me in this [place] seven altars, and make ready for me in this [place] seven bullocks and seven rams.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balak doth as Balaam hath spoken, and Balak -- Balaam also -- offereth a bullock and a ram on the altar,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Balaam saith to Balak, 'Station thyself by thy burnt-offering and I go on, it may be Jehovah doth come to meet me, and the thing which He sheweth me -- I have declared to thee;' and he goeth [to] a high place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God cometh unto Balaam, and he saith unto Him, 'The seven altars I have arranged, and I offer a bullock and a ram on the altar;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah putteth a word in the mouth of Balaam, and saith, 'Turn back unto Balak, and thus thou dost speak.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he turneth back unto him, and lo, he is standing by his burnt-offering, he and all the princes of Moab.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he taketh up his simile, and saith: 'From Aram he doth lead me -- Balak king of Moab; From mountains of the east: Come -- curse for me Jacob, And come -- be indignant [with] Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What -- do I pierce? -- God hath not pierced! And what -- am I indignant? -- Jehovah hath not been indignant!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For from the top of rocks I see it, And from heights I behold it; Lo a people! alone it doth tabernacle, And among nations doth not reckon itself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who hath counted the dust of Jacob, And the number of the fourth of Israel? Let me die the death of upright ones, And let my last end be like his!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balak saith unto Balaam, 'What hast thou done to me? to pierce mine enemies I have taken thee -- and lo, thou hast certainly blessed;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he answereth and saith, 'That which Jehovah doth put in my mouth -- it do I not take heed to speak?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balak saith unto him, 'Come, I pray thee, with me unto another place, whence thou dost see it, only its extremity thou dost see, and all of it thou dost not see, and pierce it for me thence;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he taketh him [to] the field of Zophim, unto the top of Pisgah, and buildeth seven altars, and offereth a bullock and a ram on the altar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith unto Balak, 'Station thyself here by thy burnt-offering, and I -- I meet [Him] there;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah cometh unto Balaam, and setteth a word in his mouth, and saith, 'Turn back unto Balak, and thus thou dost speak.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he cometh unto him, and lo, he is standing by his burnt-offering, and the princes of Moab with him, and Balak saith to him: 'What hath Jehovah spoken?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he taketh up his simile, and saith: 'Rise, Balak, and hear; Give ear unto me, son of Zippor!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God [is] not a man -- and lieth, And a son of man -- and repenteth! Hath He said -- and doth He not do [it]? And spoken -- and doth He not confirm it?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, to bless I have received: Yea, He blesseth, and I [can] not reverse it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath not beheld iniquity in Jacob, Nor hath He seen perverseness in Israel; Jehovah his God [is] with him, And a shout of a king [is] in him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God is bringing them out from Egypt, As the swiftness of a Reem is to him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For no enchantment [is] against Jacob, Nor divination against Israel, At the time it is said of Jacob and Israel, What hath God wrought!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, the people as a lioness riseth, And as a lion he lifteth himself up, He lieth not down till he eateth prey, And blood of pierced ones doth drink.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balak saith unto Balaam, 'Neither pierce it at all, nor bless it at all;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Balaam answereth and saith unto Balak, 'Have I not spoken unto thee, saying, All that Jehovah speaketh -- it I do?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balak saith unto Balaam, 'Come, I pray thee, I take thee unto another place; it may be it is right in the eyes of God -- to pierce it for me from thence.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balak taketh Balaam to the top of Peor, which is looking on the front of the wilderness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Balaam saith unto Balak, 'Build for me in this [place] seven altars, and make ready for me in this [place] seven bullocks and seven rams;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Balak doth as Balaam said, and he offereth a bullock and a ram on an altar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balaam seeth that [it is] good in the eyes of Jehovah to bless Israel, and he hath not gone as time by time to meet enchantments, and he setteth towards the wilderness his face;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Balaam lifteth up his eyes, and seeth Israel tabernacling, by its tribes, and the Spirit of God is upon him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he taketh up his simile, and saith: 'An affirmation of Balaam son of Beor -- And an affirmation of the man whose eyes are shut --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: An affirmation of him who is hearing sayings of God -- Who a vision of the Almighty seeth, Falling -- and eyes uncovered:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How good have been thy tents, O Jacob, Thy tabernacles, O Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As valleys they have been stretched out, As gardens by a river; As aloes Jehovah hath planted, As cedars by waters;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He maketh water flow from his buckets, And his seed [is] in many waters; And higher than Agag [is] his king, And exalted is his kingdom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God is bringing him out of Egypt; As the swiftness of a Reem is to him, He eateth up nations his adversaries, And their bones he breaketh, And [with] his arrows he smiteth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath bent, he hath lain down as a lion, And as a lioness: who doth raise him up? He who is blessing thee [is] blessed, And he who is cursing thee [is] cursed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the anger of Balak burneth against Balaam, and he striketh his hands; and Balak saith unto Balaam, 'To pierce mine enemies I called thee, and lo, thou hast certainly blessed -- these three times;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, flee for thyself unto thy place; I have said, I do greatly honour thee, and lo, Jehovah hath kept thee back from honour.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balaam saith unto Balak, 'Did I not also unto thy messengers whom thou hast sent unto me, speak, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If Balak doth give to me the fulness of his house of silver and gold, I am not able to pass over the command of Jehovah, to do good or evil of mine own heart -- that which Jehovah speaketh -- it I speak?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and, now, lo, I am going to my people; come, I counsel thee [concerning] that which this people doth to thy people, in the latter end of the days.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he taketh up his simile, and saith: 'An affirmation of Balaam son of Beor -- And an affirmation of the man whose eyes [are] shut --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: An affirmation of him who is hearing sayings of God -- And knowing knowledge of the Most High; A vision of the Almighty he seeth, Falling -- and eyes uncovered:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I see it, but not now; I behold it, but not near; A star hath proceeded from Jacob, And a sceptre hath risen from Israel, And hath smitten corners of Moab, And hath destroyed all sons of Sheth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Edom hath been a possession, And Seir hath been a possession, [for] its enemies, And Israel is doing valiantly;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And [one] doth rule out of Jacob, And hath destroyed a remnant from Ar.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he seeth Amalek, and taketh up his simile, and saith: 'A beginning of the Goyim [is] Amalek; And his latter end -- for ever he perisheth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he seeth the Kenite, and taketh up his simile, and saith: 'Enduring [is] thy dwelling, And setting in a rock thy nest,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But the Kenite is for a burning; Till when doth Asshur keep thee captive?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he taketh up his simile, and saith: 'Alas! who doth live when God doth this?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And -- ships [are] from the side of Chittim, And they have humbled Asshur, And they have humbled Eber, And it also for ever is perishing.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Balaam riseth, and goeth, and turneth back to his place, and Balak also hath gone on his way.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel dwelleth in Shittim, and the people begin to go a-whoring unto daughters of Moab,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they call for the people to the sacrifices of their gods, and the people eat, and bow themselves to their gods,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Israel is joined to Baal-Peor, and the anger of Jehovah burneth against Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'Take all the chiefs of the people, and hang them before Jehovah -- over-against the sun; and the fierceness of the anger of Jehovah doth turn back from Israel.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith unto the judges of Israel, 'Slay ye each his men who are joined to Baal-Peor.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, a man of the sons of Israel hath come, and bringeth in unto his brethren the Midianitess, before the eyes of Moses, and before the eyes of all the company of the sons of Israel, who are weeping at the opening of the tent of meeting;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Phinehas, son of Eleazar, son of Aaron, the priest, seeth, and riseth from the midst of the company, and taketh a javelin in his hand,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and goeth in after the man of Israel unto the hollow place, and pierceth them both, the man of Israel and the woman -- unto her belly, and the plague is restrained from the sons of Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the dead by the plague are four and twenty thousand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Phinehas, son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, hath turned back My fury from the sons of Israel, by his being zealous with My zeal in their midst, and I have not consumed the sons of Israel in My zeal.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Therefore say, Lo, I am giving to him My covenant of peace,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been to him and to his seed after him a covenant of a priesthood age-during, because that he hath been zealous for his God, and doth make atonement for the sons of Israel.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the name of the man of Israel who is smitten, who hath been smitten with the Midianitess, [is] Zimri son of Salu, prince of the house of a father of the Simeonite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the name of the woman who is smitten, the Midianitess, [is] Cozbi daughter of Zur, head of a people -- of the house of a father in Midian [is] he.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Distress the Midianites, and ye have smitten them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for they are adversaries to you with their frauds, [with] which they have acted fraudulently to you, concerning the matter of Peor, and concerning the matter of Cozbi, daughter of a prince of Midian, their sister, who is smitten in the day of the plague for the matter of Peor.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, after the plague, that Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, and unto Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take up the sum of all the company of the sons of Israel, from a son of twenty years and upward, by the house of their fathers, every one going out to the host in Israel.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses speaketh -- Eleazar the priest also -- with them, in the plains of Moab, by Jordan, [near] Jericho, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'From a son of twenty years and upward,' as Jehovah hath commanded Moses and the sons of Israel who are coming out from the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Reuben, first-born of Israel -- sons of Reuben: [of] Hanoch [is] the family of the Hanochite; of Pallu the family of the Palluite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of Hezron the family of the Hezronite; of Carmi the family of the Carmite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of the Reubenite, and their numbered ones are three and forty thousand and seven hundred and thirty.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the son of Pallu [is] Eliab;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the sons of Eliab [are] Nemuel and Dathan and Abiram; this [is that] Dathan and Abiram, called ones of the company, who have striven against Moses and against Aaron in the company of Korah, in their striving against Jehovah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the earth openeth her mouth, and swalloweth them and Korah, in the death of the company, in the fire consuming the two hundred and fifty men, and they become a sign;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the sons of Korah died not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Simeon by their families: of Nemuel [is] the family of the Nemuelite; of Jamin the family of the Jaminite; of Jachin the family of the Jachinite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of Zerah the family of the Zarhite; of Shaul the family of the Shaulite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of the Simeonite, two and twenty thousand and two hundred.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Gad by their families: of Zephon [is] the family of the Zephonite; of Haggi the family of the Haggite; of Shuni the family of the Shunite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of Ozni the family of the Oznite; of Eri the family of the Erite:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of Arod the family of the Arodite; of Areli the family of the Arelite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of the sons of Gad, by their numbered ones, forty thousand and five hundred.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Judah [are] Er and Onan; and Er dieth -- Onan also -- in the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Judah, by their families, are: of Shelah the family of the Shelanite; of Pharez the family of the Pharzite; of Zerah the family of the Zarhite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and sons of Pharez are: of Hezron the family of the Hezronite; of Hamul the family of the Hamulite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of Judah, by their numbered ones, six and seventy thousand and five hundred.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Issachar by their families; [of] Tola [is] the family of the Tolaite; of Pua the family of the Punite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of Jashub the family of the Jashubite; of Shimron the family of the Shimronite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of Issachar, by their numbered ones, four and sixty thousand and three hundred.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Zebulun by their families: of Sered [is] the family of the Sardite; of Elon the family of the Elonite; of Jahleel the family of the Jahleelite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of the Zebulunite by their numbered ones, sixty thousand and five hundred.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Joseph by their families [are] Manasseh and Ephraim.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Manasseh: of Machir [is] the family of the Machirite; and Machir hath begotten Gilead; of Gilead [is] the family of the Gileadite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Gilead: [of] Jeezer [is] the family of the Jeezerite; of Helek the family of the Helekite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and [of] Asriel the family of the Asrielite; and [of] Shechem the family of the Shechemite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and [of] Shemida the family of the Shemidaite; and [of] Hepher the family of the Hepherite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Zelophehad son of Hepher had no sons but daughters, and the names of the daughters of Zelophehad [are] Mahlah, and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of Manasseh, and their numbered ones [are] two and fifty thousand and seven hundred.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Ephraim by their families: of Shuthelah [is] the family of the Shuthelhite; of Becher the family of the Bachrite; of Tahan the family of the Tahanite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] sons of Shuthelah: of Eran the family of the Eranite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of the sons of Ephraim, by their numbered ones, two and thirty thousand and five hundred. These [are] sons of Joseph by their families.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Benjamin by their families: of Bela [is] the family of the Belaite; of Ashbel the family of the Ashbelite; of Ahiram the family of the Ahiramite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of Shupham the family of the Shuphamite; of Hupham the family of the Huphamite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Bela are Ard and Naaman: [of Ard is] the family of the Ardite: of Naaman the family of the Naamite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Benjamin by their families, and their numbered ones [are] five and forty thousand and six hundred.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Dan by their families: of Shuham [is] the family of the Shuhamite; these [are] families of Dan by their families;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: all the families of the Shuhamite, by their numbered ones, [are] four and sixty thousand and four hundred.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Asher by their families: of Jimna [is] the family of the Jimnite; of Jesui the family of the Jesuite; of Beriah the family of the Beriite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Of sons of Beriah: of Heber [is] the family of the Heberite; of Malchiel the family of the Malchielite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the name of the daughter of Asher [is] Sarah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of the sons of Asher, by their numbered ones, three and fifty thousand and four hundred.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Naphtali by their families: of Jahzeel [is] the family of the Jahzeelite; of Guni the family of the Gunite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of Jezer the family of the Jezerite; of Shillem the family of the Shillemite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of Naphtali by their families, and their numbered ones [are] five and forty thousand and four hundred.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] numbered ones of the sons of Israel, six hundred thousand, and a thousand, seven hundred and thirty.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'To these is the land apportioned by inheritance, by the number of names;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to the many thou dost increase their inheritance, and to the few thou dost diminish their inheritance; [to] each according to his numbered ones is given his inheritance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only by lot is the land apportioned, by the names of the tribes of their fathers they inherit;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: according to the lot is their inheritance apportioned between many and few.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] numbered ones of the Levite by their families: of Gershon [is] the family of the Gershonite; of Kohath the family of the Kohathite; of Merari the family of the Merarite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of the Levite: the family of the Libnite, the family of the Hebronite, the family of the Mahlite, the family of the Mushite, the family of the Korathite. And Kohath hath begotten Amram,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the name of Amram's wife is Jochebed, daughter of Levi, whom [one] hath born to Levi in Egypt; and she beareth to Amram Aaron, and Moses, and Miriam their sister.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And born to Aaron Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Nadab dieth -- Abihu also -- in their bringing near strange fire before Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And their numbered ones are three and twenty thousand, every male from a son of a month and upwards, for they have not numbered themselves in the midst of the sons of Israel; for an inheritance hath not been given to them in the midst of the sons of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] those numbered by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who have numbered the sons of Israel in the plains of Moab, by Jordan, [near] Jericho;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and among these there hath not been a man of those numbered by Moses, and Aaron the priest, who numbered the sons of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Jehovah said of them, 'They do certainly die in the wilderness;' and there hath not been left of them a man save Caleb son of Jephunneh, and Joshua son of Nun.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And daughters of Zelophehad son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, of the families of Manasseh son of Joseph, draw near -- and these [are] the names of his daughters, Mahlah, Noah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Tirzah --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and stand before Moses, and before Eleazar the priest, and before the princes, and all the company, at the opening of the tent of meeting, saying:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Our father died in the wilderness, and he -- he was not in the midst of the company who were met together against Jehovah in the company of Korah, but for his own sin he died, and had no sons;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: why is the name of our father withdrawn from the midst of his family because he hath no son? give to us a possession in the midst of the brethren of our father;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses bringeth near their cause before Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Rightly are the daughters of Zelophehad speaking; thou dost certainly give to them a possession of an inheritance in the midst of their father's brethren, and hast caused to pass over the inheritance of their father to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And unto the sons of Israel thou dost speak, saying, When a man dieth, and hath no son, then ye have caused his inheritance to pass over to his daughter;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if he have no daughter, then ye have given his inheritance to his brethren;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if he have no brethren, then ye have given his inheritance to his father's brethren;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if his father have no brethren, then ye have given his inheritance to his relation who is near unto him of his family, and he hath possessed it;' and it hath been to the sons of Israel for a statute of judgment, as Jehovah hath commanded Moses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'Go up unto this mount Abarim, and see the land which I have given to the sons of Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast seen it, and thou hast been gathered unto thy people, also thou, as Aaron thy brother hath been gathered,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because ye provoked My mouth in the wilderness of Zin, in the strife of the company -- to sanctify Me at the waters before their eyes;' they [are] waters of Meribah, in Kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses speaketh unto Jehovah, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah -- God of the spirits of all flesh -- appoint a man over the company,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who goeth out before them, and who cometh in before them, and who taketh them out, and who bringeth them in, and the company of Jehovah is not as sheep which have no shepherd.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Moses, 'Take to thee Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom [is] the Spirit, and thou hast laid thine hand upon him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hast caused him to stand before Eleazar the priest, and before all the company, and hast charged him before their eyes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hast put of thine honour upon him, so that all the company of the sons of Israel do hearken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And before Eleazar the priest he standeth, and he hath asked for him by the judgment of the Lights before Jehovah; at His word they go out, and at His word they come in; he, and all the sons of Israel with him, even all the company.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses doth as Jehovah hath commanded him, and taketh Joshua, and causeth him to stand before Eleazar the priest, and before all the company,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and layeth his hands upon him, and chargeth him, as Jehovah hath spoken by the hand of Moses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Command the sons of Israel, and thou hast said unto them, My offering, My bread for My fire-offerings, My sweet fragrance, ye take heed to bring near to Me in its appointed season.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou hast said to them, This [is] the fire-offering which ye bring near to Jehovah: two lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones, daily, a continual burnt-offering;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the one lamb thou preparest in the morning, and the second lamb thou preparest between the evenings;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a tenth of the ephah of flour for a present, mixed with beaten oil, a fourth of the hin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a continual burnt-offering, which was made in mount Sinai, for sweet fragrance, a fire-offering to Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and its libation, a fourth of the hin for the one lamb; in the sanctuary cause thou a libation of strong drink to be poured out to Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the second lamb thou dost prepare between the evenings; as the present of the morning, and as its libation thou preparest -- a fire-offering, a sweet fragrance to Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And on the sabbath-day, two lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones, and two-tenth deals of flour, a present, mixed with oil, and its libation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the burnt-offering of the sabbath in its sabbath, besides the continual burnt-offering and its libation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And in the beginnings of your months ye bring near a burnt-offering to Jehovah: two bullocks, sons of the herd, and one ram, seven lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and three-tenth deals of flour, a present, mixed with oil, for the one bullock, and two-tenth deals of flour, a present, mixed with oil, for the one ram;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a several tenth deal of flour, a present, mixed with oil, for the one lamb; a burnt-offering, a sweet fragrance, a fire-offering to Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their libations are a half of the hin to a bullock, and a third of the hin to a ram, and a fourth of the hin to a lamb, of wine; this [is] the burnt-offering of every month for the months of the year;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one kid of the goats for a sin-offering to Jehovah; besides the continual burnt-offering it is prepared, and its libation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And in the first month, in the fourteenth day of the month, [is] the passover to Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in the fifteenth day of this month [is] a festival, seven days unleavened food is eaten;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in the first day [is] an holy convocation, ye do no servile work,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have brought near a fire-offering, a burnt-offering to Jehovah: two bullocks, sons of the herd, and one ram, and seven lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones they are for you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their present, flour mixed with oil, three-tenth deals for a bullock, and two-tenth deals for a ram ye do prepare;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a several tenth deal thou preparest for the one lamb, for the seven lambs,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one goat, a sin-offering, to make atonement for you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Apart from the burnt-offering of the morning, which [is] for the continual burnt-offering, ye prepare these;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: according to these ye prepare daily, seven days, bread of a fire-offering, a sweet fragrance, to Jehovah; besides the continual burnt-offering it is prepared, and its libation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on the seventh day a holy convocation ye have, ye do no servile work.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And in the day of the first-fruits, in your bringing near a new present to Jehovah, in your weeks, a holy convocation ye have; ye do no servile work;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have brought near a burnt-offering for sweet fragrance to Jehovah: two bullocks, sons of the herd, one ram, seven lambs, sons of a year,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their present, flour mixed with oil, three-tenth deals to the one bullock, two-tenth deals to the one ram,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a several tenth deal to the one lamb, for the seven lambs;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: one kid of the goats to make atonement for you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: apart from the continual burnt-offering and its present ye prepare [them] (perfect ones they are for you) and their libations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And in the seventh month, in the first of the month, a holy convocation ye have, ye do no servile work; a day of shouting it is to you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have prepared a burnt-offering, for sweet fragrance to Jehovah: one bullock, a son of the herd, one ram, seven lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their present, flour mixed with oil, three-tenth deals for the bullock, two-tenth deals for the ram,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one-tenth deal for the one lamb, for the seven lambs;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one kid of the goats, a sin-offering, to make atonement for you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: apart from the burnt-offering of the month, and its present, and the continual burnt-offering, and its present, and their libations, according to their ordinance, for sweet fragrance, a fire-offering to Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And on the tenth of this seventh month a holy convocation ye have, and ye have humbled your souls; ye do no work;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have brought near a burnt-offering to Jehovah, a sweet fragrance, one bullock, a son of the herd, one ram, seven lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones they are for you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their present, flour mixed with oil, three-tenth deals for the bullock, two-tenth deals for the one ram,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a several tenth deal for the one lamb, for the seven lambs,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: one kid of the goats, a sin-offering; apart from the sin-offering of the atonements, and the continual burnt-offering, and its present, and their libations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And on the fifteenth day of the seventh month a holy convocation ye have; ye do no servile work; and ye have celebrated a festival to Jehovah seven days,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and have brought near a burnt-offering, a fire-offering, a sweet fragrance, to Jehovah; thirteen bullocks, sons of the herd, two rams, fourteen lambs, sons of a year; perfect ones they are;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their present, flour mixed with oil, three-tenth deals to the one bullock, for the thirteen bullocks, two-tenth deals to the one ram, for the two rams,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a several tenth deal to the one lamb, for the fourteen lambs,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one kid of the goats, a sin-offering; apart from the continual burnt-offering, its present, and its libation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And on the second day twelve bullocks, sons of the herd, two rams, fourteen lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their present, and their libations, for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the sheep, in their number, according to the ordinance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one kid of the goats, a sin-offering; apart from the continual burnt-offering, and its present, and their libations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And on the third day eleven bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their present, and their libations, for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, in their number, according to the ordinance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one goat, a sin-offering; apart from the continual burnt-offering, and its present, and its libation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And on the fourth day ten bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: their present, and their libations, for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, in their number, according to the ordinance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one kid of the goats, a sin-offering, apart from the continual burnt-offering, its present, and its libation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And on the fifth day nine bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their present, and their libations, for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, in their number, according to the ordinance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one goat, a sin-offering; apart from the continual burnt-offering, and its present, and its libation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And on the sixth day eight bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their present, and their libations, for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, in their number, according to the ordinance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one goat, a sin-offering; apart from the continual burnt-offering, its present, and its libation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And on the seventh day seven bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their present, and their libations, for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, in their number, according to the ordinance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one goat, a sin-offering; apart from the continual burnt-offering, its present, and its libation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'On the eighth day a restraint ye have, ye do no servile work;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have brought near a burnt-offering, a fire-offering, a sweet fragrance, to Jehovah; one bullock, one ram, seven lambs, sons of a year, perfect ones;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: their present, and their libations, for the bullock, for the ram, and for the lambs, in their number, according to the ordinance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one goat, a sin-offering; apart from the continual burnt-offering, and its present, and its libation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'These ye prepare to Jehovah in your appointed seasons, apart from your vows, and your free-will offerings, for your burnt-offerings, and for your presents, and for your libations, and for your peace-offerings.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith unto the sons of Israel according to all that Jehovah hath commanded Moses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses speaketh unto the heads of the tribes of the sons of Israel, saying, 'This [is] the thing which Jehovah hath commanded:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When a man voweth a vow to Jehovah, or hath sworn an oath to bind a bond on his soul, he doth not pollute his word; according to all that is going out from his mouth he doth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when a woman voweth a vow to Jehovah, and hath bound a bond in the house of her father in her youth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and her father hath heard her vow, and her bond which she hath bound on her soul, and her father hath kept silent at her, then have all her vows been established, and every bond which she hath bound on her soul is established.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if her father hath disallowed her in the day of his hearing, none of her vows and her bonds which she hath bound on her soul is established, and Jehovah is propitious to her, for her father hath disallowed her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if she be at all to a husband, and her vows [are] on her, or a wrongful utterance [on] her lips, which she hath bound on her soul,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and her husband hath heard, and in the day of his hearing, he hath kept silent at her, then have her vows been established, and her bonds which she hath bound on her soul are established.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if in the day of her husband's hearing he disalloweth her, then he hath broken her vow which [is] on her, and the wrongful utterance of her lips which she hath bound on her soul, and Jehovah is propitious to her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'As to the vow of a widow or cast-out woman, all that she hath bound on her soul is established on her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if [in] the house of her husband she hath vowed, or hath bound a bond on her soul with an oath,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and her husband hath heard, and hath kept silent at her -- he hath not disallowed her -- then have all her vows been established, and every bond which she hath bound on her soul is established.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if her husband doth certainly break them in the day of his hearing, none of the outgoing of her lips concerning her vows, or concerning the bond of her soul, is established -- her husband hath broken them -- and Jehovah is propitious to her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Every vow and every oath -- a bond to humble a soul -- her husband doth establish it, or her husband doth break it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if her husband certainly keep silent at her, from day unto day, then he hath established all her vows, or all her bonds which [are] upon her; he hath established them, for he hath kept silent at her in the day of his hearing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if he doth at all break them after his hearing, then he hath borne her iniquity.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] the statutes which Jehovah hath commanded Moses between a man and his wife, between a father and his daughter, in her youth, [in] the house of her father.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Execute the vengeance of the sons of Israel against the Midianites -- afterwards thou art gathered unto thy people.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses speaketh unto the people, saying, 'Be ye armed some of you for the host, and they are against Midian, to put the vengeance of Jehovah on Midian;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a thousand for a tribe -- a thousand for a tribe, to all the tribes of Israel -- ye do send to the host.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there are given out of the thousands of Israel a thousand for a tribe, twelve thousand armed ones of the host;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses sendeth them, a thousand for a tribe, to the host, them and Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest, to the host; and the holy vessels, and the trumpets of the shouting, in his hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they war against Midian, as Jehovah hath commanded Moses, and slay every male;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the kings of Midian they have slain, besides their pierced ones, Evi, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba, five kings of Midian; and Balaam son of Beor, they have slain with the sword.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Israel take captive the women of Midian, and their infants; and all their cattle, and all their substance, and all their wealth they have plundered;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all their cities, with their habitations, and all their towers, they have burnt with fire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they take all the spoil, and all the prey, among man and among beast;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they bring in, unto Moses, and unto Eleazar the priest, and unto the company of the sons of Israel, the captives, and the prey, and the spoil, unto the camp, unto the plains of Moab, which [are] by Jordan, [near] Jericho.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses, and Eleazar the priest, and all the princes of the company, go out to meet them, unto the outside of the camp,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses is wroth against the inspectors of the force, chiefs of the thousands, and chiefs of the hundreds, who are coming in from the host of the battle.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith unto them, 'Have ye kept alive every female?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lo, they -- they have been to the sons of Israel, through the word of Balaam, to cause a trespass against Jehovah in the matter of Peor, and the plague is in the company of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, slay ye every male among the infants, yea, every woman known of man by the lying of a male ye have slain;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the infants among the women, who have not known the lying of a male, ye have kept alive for yourselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye, encamp ye at the outside of the camp seven days -- any who hath slain a person, and any who hath come against a pierced one, ye cleanse yourselves on the third day, and on the seventh day -- ye and your captives;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and every garment, and every skin vessel, and every work of goats' [hair], and every wooden vessel, ye yourselves cleanse.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Eleazar the priest saith unto the men of the host who go in to battle, 'This [is] the statute of the law which Jehovah hath commanded Moses:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: only, the gold, and the silver, the brass, the iron, the tin, and the lead,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: every thing which may go into fire, ye cause to pass over through fire, and it hath been clean; only, with the water of separation it is cleansed, and all that may not go into fire, ye cause to pass over through water;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have washed your garments on the seventh day, and have been clean, and afterwards ye come in unto the camp.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take up the sum of the prey of the captives, among man and among beast, thou, and Eleazar the priest, and the heads of the fathers of the company;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast halved the prey between those handling the battle who go out to the host and all the company;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast raised a tribute to Jehovah from the men of war, who go out to the host, one body out of five hundred, of man, and of the herd, and of the asses, and of the flock;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: from their half ye do take, and thou hast given to Eleazar the priest -- the heave-offering of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And from the sons of Israel's half thou dost take one possession out of fifty, of man, of the herd, of the asses, and of the flock, of all the cattle, and thou hast given them to the Levites keeping the charge of the tabernacle of Jehovah.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses doth -- Eleazar the priest also -- as Jehovah hath commanded Moses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the prey, the residue of the spoil which the people of the host have spoiled, is of the flock six hundred thousand, and seventy thousand, and five thousand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of the herd two and seventy thousand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of asses one and sixty thousand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of human beings -- of the women who have not known the lying of a male -- all the persons [are] two and thirty thousand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the half -- the portion of those who go out into the host -- the number of the flock is three hundred thousand, and thirty thousand, and seven thousand and five hundred.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the tribute to Jehovah of the sheep is six hundred five and seventy;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the herd [is] six and thirty thousand, and their tribute to Jehovah [is] two and seventy;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the asses [are] thirty thousand and five hundred, and their tribute to Jehovah [is] one and sixty;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the human beings [are] sixteen thousand, and their tribute to Jehovah [is] two and thirty persons.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses giveth the tribute -- Jehovah's heave-offering -- to Eleazar the priest, as Jehovah hath commanded Moses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And of the sons of Israel's half, which Moses halved from the men who war --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the company's half is, of the flock three hundred thousand, and thirty thousand, seven thousand and five hundred;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of the herd six and thirty thousand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of asses thirty thousand and five hundred;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of human beings sixteen thousand --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Moses taketh from the sons of Israel's half the one possession from the fifty, of man and of beast, and giveth them to the Levites keeping the charge of the tabernacle of Jehovah, as Jehovah hath commanded Moses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the inspectors whom the thousands of the host hath, (heads of the thousands and heads of the hundreds), draw near unto Moses,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they say unto Moses, 'Thy servants have taken up the sum of the men of war who [are] with us, and not a man of us hath been missed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we bring near Jehovah's offering, each that which he hath found, vessels of gold -- chain, and bracelet, seal-ring, [ear] -ring, and bead -- to make atonement for ourselves before Jehovah.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses receiveth -- Eleazar the priest also -- the gold from them, every made vessel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the gold of the heave-offering which they have lifted up to Jehovah is sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels, from heads of the thousands, and from heads of the hundreds;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (the men of the host have spoiled each for himself);

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses taketh -- Eleazar the priest also -- the gold from the heads of the thousands and of the hundreds, and they bring it in unto the tent of meeting -- a memorial for the sons of Israel before Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And much cattle hath been to the sons of Reuben and to the sons of Gad, very many; and they see the land of Jazer, and the land of Gilead, and lo, the place [is] a place [for] cattle;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the sons of Gad, and the sons of Reuben, come in and speak unto Moses, and unto Eleazar the priest, and unto the princes of the company, saying:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ataroth, and Dibon, and Jazer, and Nimrah, and Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Shebam, and Nebo, and Beon --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the land which Jehovah hath smitten before the company of Israel, is a land for cattle, and thy servants have cattle.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say, 'If we have found grace in thine eyes, let this land be given to thy servants for a possession; cause us not to pass over the Jordan.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Reuben, 'Do your brethren go in to the battle, and ye -- do ye sit here?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and why discourage ye the heart of the sons of Israel from passing over unto the land which Jehovah hath given to them?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thus did your fathers in my sending them from Kadesh-Barnea to see the land;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they go up unto the valley of Eshcol, and see the land, and discourage the heart of the sons of Israel so as not to go in unto the land which Jehovah hath given to them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the anger of Jehovah burneth in that day, and He sweareth, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They do not see -- the men who are coming up out of Egypt from a son of twenty years and upward -- the ground which I have sworn to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, for they have not been fully after Me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: save Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenezite, and Joshua son of Nun, for they have been fully after Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the anger of Jehovah burneth against Israel, and He causeth them to wander in the wilderness forty years, until the consumption of all the generation which is doing the evil thing in the eyes of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And lo, ye have risen in the stead of your fathers, an increase of men -- sinners, to add yet to the fury of the anger of Jehovah toward Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: when ye turn back from after Him, then He hath added yet to leave him in the wilderness, and ye have done corruptly to all this people.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they come nigh unto him, and say, 'Folds for the flock we build for our cattle here, and cities for our infants;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we -- we are armed hasting before the sons of Israel till that we have brought them in unto their place; and our infants have dwelt in the cities of defence because of the inhabitants of the land;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: we do not turn back unto our houses till the sons of Israel have inherited each his inheritance,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for we do not inherit with them beyond the Jordan and yonder, for our inheritance hath come unto us beyond the Jordan at the [sun] -rising.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses saith unto them, 'If ye do this thing: if ye are armed before Jehovah for battle,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and every armed one of you hath passed over the Jordan before Jehovah, till his dispossessing His enemies from before Him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the land hath been subdued before Jehovah -- then afterwards ye do turn back, and have been acquitted by Jehovah, and by Israel; and this land hath been to you for a possession before Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if ye do not so, lo, ye have sinned against Jehovah, and know ye your sin, that it doth find you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: build for yourselves cities for your infants, and folds for your flock, and that which is going out from your mouth do ye.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben speak unto Moses, saying, 'Thy servants do as my lord is commanding;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: our infants, our wives, our cattle, and all our beasts, are there in cities of Gilead,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thy servants pass over, every armed one of the host, before Jehovah, to battle, as my lord is saying.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses commandeth concerning them Eleazar the priest, and Joshua son of Nun, and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the sons of Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses saith unto them, 'If the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben pass over with you the Jordan, every one armed for battle, before Jehovah, and the land hath been subdued before you, then ye have given to them the land of Gilead for a possession;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if they do not pass over armed with you, then they have possessions in your midst in the land of Canaan.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben answer, saying, 'That which Jehovah hath spoken unto thy servants -- so we do;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: we -- we pass over armed before Jehovah [to] the land of Canaan, and with us [is] the possession of our inheritance beyond the Jordan.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Moses giveth to them, to the sons of Gad, and to the sons of Reuben, and to the half of the tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorite, and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan, the land by its cities, in the borders, the cities of the land round about.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Gad build Dihon, and Ataroth, and Aroer,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Atroth, Shophan, and Jaazer, and Jogbehah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Beth-Nimrah, and Beth-Haran, cities of defence, and sheepfolds.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Reuben have build Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Kirjathaim,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Nebo, and Baal-Meon (changed in name), and Shibmah, and they call by [these] names the names of the cities which they have built.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Machir son of Manasseh go to Gilead, and capture it, and dispossess the Amorite, who [is] in it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses giveth Gilead to Machir son of Manasseh, and he dwelleth in it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jair son of Manasseh hath gone and captureth their towns, and calleth them 'Towns of Jair;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Nobah hath gone and captureth Kenath, and its villages, and calleth it Nobah, by his own name.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] journeys of the sons of Israel who have come out of the land of Egypt, by their hosts, by the hand of Moses and Aaron;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Moses writeth their outgoings, by their journeys, by the command of Jehovah; and these [are] their journeys, by their outgoings:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month, on the morrow of the passover have the sons of Israel gone out with a high hand, before the eyes of all the Egyptians --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Egyptians are burying those whom Jehovah hath smitten among them, every first-born, and on their gods hath Jehovah done judgments --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the sons of Israel journey from Rameses, and encamp in Succoth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Succoth, and encamp in Etham, which [is] in the extremity of the wilderness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Etham, and turn back on Pi-Hahiroth, which [is] on the front of Baal-Zephon, and they encamp before Migdol.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Pi-Hahiroth, and pass over through the midst of the sea, into the wilderness, and go a journey of three days in the wilderness of Etham, and encamp in Marah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Marah, and come in to Elim, and in Elim [are] twelve fountains of waters, and seventy palm trees, and they encamp there;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Elim, and encamp by the Red Sea.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from the Red Sea, and encamp in the wilderness of Sin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from the wilderness of Sin, and encamp in Dophkah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Dophkah, and encamp in Alush;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Alush, and encamp in Rephidim; and there was there no water for the people to drink.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Rephidim, and encamp in the wilderness of Sinai;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from the wilderness of Sinai, and encamp in Kibroth-Hattaavah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Kibroth-Hattaavah, and encamp in Hazeroth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Hazeroth, and encamp in Rithmah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Rithmah, and encamp in Rimmon-Parez;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Rimmon-Parez, and encamp in Libnah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Libnah, and encamp in Rissah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Rissah, and encamp in Kehelathah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Kehelathah, and encamp in mount Shapher;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from mount Shapher, and encamp in Haradah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Haradah, and encamp in Makheloth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Makheloth, and encamp in Tahath.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Tahath, and encamp in Tarah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Tarah, and encamp in Mithcah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Mithcah, and encamp in Hashmonah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Hashmonah, and encamp in Moseroth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Moseroth, and encamp in Bene-Jaakan;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Bene-Jaakan, and encamp at Hor-Hagidgad.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Hor-Hagidgad, and encamp in Jotbathah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Jotbathah, and encamp in Ebronah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Ebronah, and encamp in Ezion-Gaber;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Ezion-Gaber, and encamp in the wilderness of Zin, which [is] Kadesh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Kadesh, and encamp in mount Hor, in the extremity of the land of Edom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Aaron the priest goeth up unto mount Hor, by the command of Jehovah, and dieth there, in the fortieth year of the going out of the sons of Israel from the land of Egypt, in the fifth month, on the first of the month;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Aaron [is] a son of a hundred and twenty and three years in his dying in mount Hor.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Canaanite -- king Arad -- who is dwelling in the south, in the land of Canaan, heareth of the coming of the sons of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from mount Hor, and encamp in Zalmonah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Zalmonah, and encamp in Punon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Punon, and encamp in Oboth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Oboth, and encamp in Ije-Abarim, in the border of Moab.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Iim, and encamp in Dibon-Gad;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from Dibon-Gad, and encamp in Almon-Diblathaim.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Almon-Diblathaim, and encamp in the mountains of Abarim, before Nebo;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey from the mountains of Abarim, and encamp in the plains of Moab, by Jordan, [near] Jericho.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they encamp by the Jordan from Beth-Jeshimoth, unto Abel-Shittim, in the plains of Moab.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, in the plains of Moab, by Jordan, [near] Jericho, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou hast said unto them, When ye are passing over the Jordan unto the land of Canaan,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then ye have dispossessed all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and have destroyed all their imagery, yea, all their molten images ye destroy, and all their high places ye lay waste,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have possessed the land, and dwelt in it, for to you I have given the land -- to possess it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye have inherited the land by lot, by your families; to the many ye increase their inheritance, and to the few ye diminish their inheritance; whither the lot goeth out to him, it is his; by the tribes of your fathers ye inherit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if ye do not dispossess the inhabitants of the land from before you, then it hath been, those whom ye let remain of them, [are] for pricks in your eyes, and for thorns in your sides, and they have distressed you on the land in which ye are dwelling,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath come to pass, as I thought to do to them -- I do to you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah speaketh unto Moses, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Command the sons of Israel, and thou hast said unto them, When ye are coming in unto the land of Canaan -- this [is] the land which falleth to you by inheritance, the land of Canaan, by its borders --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then hath the south quarter been to you from the wilderness of Zin, by the sides of Edom, yea, the south border hath been to you from the extremity of the Salt Sea, eastward;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
